_readme: Ground-truth dataset for parser evaluation. One entry per plan. Filled from the drawing's own text + user answers. Used by batch_eval scorecards now; later = labelled dataset for the Phase-2 fine-tune flywheel.

FLOOR PLAN.pdf.overall_ft: USER TO CONFIRM from drawing dimension text (parser says 113.9 x 35.6 after junk fix; old 122x65 was junk-inflated)

plan1.dwg.notes: CAD DWG (AutoCAD 2013-2017). Units INCHES (confirmed: 29ft dim = 348 units). v1.1 CAD path: DWG->DXF->layered-PDF->pdf_vector.parse(ppf_hint). Test DXFs here came via Aspose EVAL (watermark X + partial entities) - libredwg in prod converts fully.

plan_roomdim_342.pdf (tests/fixtures).source: internet CAD PDF, no column layer, no on-line dims — scale from room-dimension text (9'0"X12'0")

plan_unnamed_layers_343.pdf (tests/fixtu.source: internet CAD PDF — real walls on unnamed layer '6'; named wall layers are decoys

_data_policy: plans/ = raw drawings (gitignored, confidential). ground_truth.json + INDEX.txt = the LABELS, small text, tracked in git so every change is versioned and reversible - nothing gets messed up. batch_eval reads labels, never writes them; parser outputs go to out/ (gitignored). Later: human-corrected scene.json snapshots join this file as training pairs (Phase-2 flywheel).

W3.sill_note: 3'6" (1050 mm) fixed above kitchen counter datum - CONFIRMED 2026-07-10

default_opening_legend.notes: door widths D1-D3 (2.5-3.5 ft) double as the door-width prior for multi-signal scale estimation

_cad_upload_v1_1.what: upload accepts .dxf/.dwg (+ existing pdf/png/jpg): server/cad_vector.py re-draws DXF into an in-memory layered PDF (OCG = DXF layer names) and the standard pdf_vector engine parses it with ppf_hint = EXACT scale from CAD units (no guessing)
_cad_upload_v1_1.units_rule: $INSUNITS is only a candidate (often wrong; ezdxf defaults new files to metres) - every unit guess is scored: building width 12-400 ft + dominant column 0.5-2.2 ft
_cad_upload_v1_1.dwg_rule: dwg2dxf (LibreDWG) looked up on PATH or DWG_CONVERTER env; Dockerfile builds it best-effort; absent -> friendly 'Save As DXF' 422 message
_cad_upload_v1_1.columns_rule: scale_src=cad_units accepts any column 9-24in (12in-only rule kept for PDFs)
